title: apluda mutica var. major
intro: Apluda mutica var. major, also known as the Indian grass, is a plant species that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving joint pain, and treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Apluda mutica var. major can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional medicine to address various health concerns.
